blurb: Roster, comment, investor, price-tag, stat and agent cards — the flat white surfaces with hard navy offset shadows that carry every profile and data block on the site.

components.name: Roster card
components.blurb: A single creator-position card from the campaign roster: duotoned photo avatar, handle, follower meta and XRS score. Driven by a CSS class for its anchor and picked states; tapping the live card toggles .picked.

components.name: Comment card
components.blurb: A live-feed comment chip: audience handle, the comment text and a sentiment tag. The tag comes in three CSS-driven variants — positive, watch (intent) and spike (velocity). Includes the live three-column scrolling feed.
components.html:
@rio.climbs · audience
been waiting for someone honest about sizing. ordering the 9.5
▲ POSITIVE
@vega.gardens · audience
genuine question — do these hold up in rain? asking before I buy
◎ INTENT SIGNAL
@mars.climbs · audience
third creator I've seen with these this week 👀
⚡ VELOCITY SPIKE

components.name: Investor card
components.blurb: A bordered content block from the investor section — an uppercase orange .t title plus key-value rows or a paragraph. Shown inside a dark .vg wrapper so the title and text pick up the correct theme colors.
components.html:
Revenue model
Exchange membershipAnnual
SeatsPer planner
Campaign credits% of tranche
MSR commitments$15–50M / season
The flywheel

More creators → better matches → better tranche results → bigger MSRs → more demand for creators. Every campaign trains the XRS model; the model is the switching cost. Both sides arrive for liquidity and stay for the score.

components.name: Price tag
components.blurb: The hanging price-tag component: a string and an offset accent slab behind a flat tag with a punch hole, title, Orbital logo lockup, URL row and a barcode bar. Wrapped in .vg so it hangs against the dark theme.
components.html:
One system,
every surface.
ORBITAL
www.orbital.design↗

components.name: Stat card
components.blurb: A glass-shell statistic card: a big mono .num with a highlighted <em> figure (re-themed to a neon-yellow marker swatch) over a small descriptive .lbl.
components.html:
3.7×
Better ROI reported with AI-matched creator selection vs. manual picks (Influencer Marketing Hub, 2025)

components.name: Agent face card
components.blurb: A face-picker tile from the AI agent row: a monogram .face avatar, the agent .nm name and a .role caption. Lives inside an .agents flex row. The CSS still ships even though the picker was removed from the live flow.
